summary:
STRATEGIC SPIRITUAL SOWING: Brandon explains the concept of sowing financial and spiritual seeds into specific types of ministry grounds, such as prayer warrior, prophetic, healing, and scholar grounds. He emphasizes that believers should intentionally give to ministries that possess the specific anointing or covering they need in their own lives. By evaluating the fruit of these ministries, believers can ensure they are partnering with authentic spiritual leaders and protecting themselves from the devourer when certain grounds fail to produce tangible results.

ENDURANCE AND WALKING IN LOVE: Brandon discusses the reality of facing persecution and dealing with critics within and outside the Christian community. Instead of responding with bitterness or defending his prophetic calling, he chooses to walk in love and pray for his detractors' marriages, health, and ministries. He believes that true ministries do not need to constantly announce their titles, as their spiritual fruit and authentic anointing will naturally display who they are, much like an apple or an orange reveals its true nature to anyone who tastes it.

PERSONAL SPIRITUAL RESPONSIBILITY: Brandon urges believers to take proactive responsibility for their spiritual growth rather than relying exclusively on weekly church services or their pastors for spiritual nourishment. He describes himself and his wife Diana as spiritual scavengers who actively seek out impactful sermons and deeply meditate on the Bible, particularly focusing on the red letters of Jesus, Psalms, Proverbs, and the Book of John. He shares that due to his learning disabilities, Diana helps him read, and he relies on audio Bibles, emphasizing that believers must immerse themselves in the living Word to survive the spiritually dark end times.

DELIVERANCE AND THE SWORD OF THE SPIRIT: Brandon highlights the critical role of the Bible in achieving true freedom from demonic attachments and fleshly addictions, such as gambling, alcohol, or pornography. He warns that believers must not dabble in worldly temptations or occult practices like tarot card reading, as opening doors to the devil allows demonic spirits to attach to the soulish realm. He insists that simply praying in the Spirit is not enough to defeat the enemy; believers must actively speak and quote the Word of God as a two-edged sword to cut down demonic oppression, renew their minds, and walk in total deliverance.

DIVINE ECONOMY AND COMING JUDGMENTS: Brandon warns of approaching global shakings, pestilences, severe storms, and economic uncertainties, yet he encourages believers to remain fearless by standing on the promises of protection found in Psalm 91. He refuses to participate in or worry about the world's failing economic systems, choosing instead to rely on God's divine economy of supernatural multiplication and the financial blessings outlined in Deuteronomy 28. By exercising consistent faith and trusting that Christ has redeemed believers from the curse of the law, he asserts that Christians are equipped to thrive and experience abundant provision even in hard times.

UNITY IN THE BODY OF CHRIST: Brandon compares the diverse gifts within the Christian church to a team of superheroes like the Avengers working together to defeat evil. He explains that believers possess unique spiritual abilities, such as healing, prophecy, or teaching, which are meant to complement rather than compete with one another. By joining forces, appreciating each individual's specific calling without jealousy, and forming a super team, the body of Christ can effectively combat the devil and fulfill its ultimate mission on earth.
rawData: [August 23, 2025]-[Going over some things - Diana said something today]-[https://youtu.be/zCy_S-eSX1c]-[Hey, good morning family. Praise the Lord. Thank you for joining me again today. Well, I have Diana with me. I told her, I said, 'I've already made this video. I think this is the third time.' I tried to do it without her because she was trying to do emails and everything, and she had so much work to do and the kids are homeschooling. And I told her, I said, 'I can't do the video without you, baby. I really need you to do this. Just put on a little something and just come sit by me.' So I told her, I said, 'You help me out.' But it's the truth. When you're talking to a lens of a camera, and you're pushing in the spirit realm, and after doing this video, Braden's probably like, 'You know,' I'm like, 'I'm sorry. I just need your mama.' So, I'm thankful for you. But we're excited about hitting 560,000 people. Isn't that amazing? God is so good. 56,700 is what Braden said. Prayers. That's exactly right. Not people—prayer warriors, veterans and new beginners. And then on our China channel, we have 24,000. Our Spanish channel, we have 15,000. On our South Korean channel now, we have 21,000, and so on and so on. He said, 'Dad, we have three channels that are over 20,000 and above, and then the rest of them are catching up. They're almost there.' And so, we just want to give all the glory to God about what he's done in this time frame of our YouTube existence. Isn't Jesus wonderful? He is. We set out to do this and it was all about talking about tribulation when we started two and a half years ago. It's been two and a half years. Two and a half years we've been doing this, and the Lord said, 'I want you to start going into all the world and telling people that I'm coming on YouTube.' And I had no idea that it would do exactly what it's done. I had a vision inside of what the Lord told us. But I just want to tell every one of y'all, thank you. Thank you. Thank you for liking, thank you for subscribing, thank you for being a part of this beautiful YouTube community. It's such a blessing. We love y'all so much. Truly, it's changed our lives. This has changed our lives. Seeing people in public and seeing people at church, they come from everywhere. Everybody's wanting a touch from Jesus. And we see the hunger and the desperation on people. We really do. And we're all trying to find it ourselves. We're all going up the river together and we're like, 'Hey, come on, get on the raft with us and let's paddle. We're going to get there.' And we know that there's a great revival about to take place. We know there is. But people are wanting to know about Diana and I, what we believe in tithing and sowing and things like that, and sowing to certain ground. And Diana and I, we were praying one day. We were in the car, and the Lord started talking to us about harvest ground, harvester ground, scholar ground, and warrior ground. That not all ground produces the same. But he started talking to us about when you sow into certain grounds, you reap back that covering, that harvest of that covering. And so in our line of work doing this now, we need a lot of prayer covering. And so we lined our ministry up with prayer warriors. We're a prayer channel. Prayer changes things. That's who we are. So we jumped in the boat with a bunch of prayer warriors. Candace and Billye Brim and their daughter. They're some of the greatest prayer warriors of this generation. I'm telling you, I don't know people who pray like these people. They pray on another level. And when they pray, you know when they're praying for you. So, and the other part of our vein of what God's called us to do is the prophetic and hearing and seeing watchmen and doing things that the Lord's called us to do. So, we find ground like that and we sow into it. We sow into prayer warrior ground, covering of prayer, because when you're in this, we've dealt with a lot of spiritual warfare. We sow into all the grounds. Yeah, we sow into all the ground, it's just where we feel like we need it. Sometimes you feel like you're lacking more in one area when we pray about where to sow. But it is specific. We are strategic in where we sow. Because it may be that we're going through certain things that are more than others on that day, and we say, 'I feel like we should give x amount of dollars to this ministry because it could be like prayer warrior ground.' It's like I saw Sister Billye and them. They're like Vikings in the spirit realm. They're like, 'Where's the devil?' You know, you go after the devil. You know, it's like these people have that authority. They walk in the authority, talking about the authority of the believer and taking your rights according to Ephesians and exercising that, and you can feel it when you sow into their ground. You feel that covering. It's amazing, isn't it? So then you go, 'Okay, well, I've got that base covered, but I need a little bit of because God's called us into the healing ministry.' So we find a healing ministry and we're like, 'Who's a healing ministry that we really can trust, that you know that there's fruit behind their ministry?' And that's Billy Burke. So we say, 'Well, let's sow a little seed into Billy Burke's ministry.' So as you give into that, there's a blessing that comes from that, and I believe that covering comes over you. And it's like people go, 'Brandon, I have a problem with hearing the voice of God.' Well, if I hear the voice of God and you have a problem with that, I sow into what? I sow by faith into the prophetic, and I say, 'Here's seed into the prophetic ministry.' And I'm sowing this by faith that my ears are going to become more attuned, the prophetic antenna, hearing the voice of the Father, because he said in his word that we would hear his voice, and a stranger's voice we wouldn't follow. And as I sow, I sow intentionally into that, praying for the covering of that to come on me. I say, 'Lord, I thank you that as I sow into the prophetic, Father, I thank you, Lord, that that's coming on me and my household because blessings come down.' And I've noticed if you need wisdom in scripture, I sow into scholar ground, which is people who are real educated and teachers of the gospel that teach. Some ministries have a gift of teaching, and you go there and you listen to them and you go, 'Man, I got a lot out of that. That was a lot of rhema word.' Like Brother Hagin when he was on the earth, he was a teacher but he walked in the prophetic also. So, in the past when he was here on the earth—now he's not here no more—but I would sow into him directly, whatever you could give into that or whatever. You get what I'm saying, y'all. Wherever you have a need, you sow a seed. If I need covering of prayer, I sow into prayer. If you feel like you're under spiritual attack, does it make sense? Prayer ministries, prayer things like that, you sow into that. And then if you have a need and I need to hear the Lord more, I sow into the prophetic. If I need healing, I sow into healing ministries. If I sow into financial, I look at the people who are bringing in a lot of souls. Who's bringing in the harvest? So I sow into—for 'he that wins souls is wise.' So, I see people's ministries that have a financial blessing on them that bring in a lot of souls. Like Billy Graham was one when he was alive, okay? There's a lot of souls that came to him. People go, 'Well, he was a Freemason.' I don't know about all that stuff. I'm telling you who brings a lot of people to Jesus. That's what I don't get into—all the flakiness of who they were, the way they shake hands with the president, and doing stuff. I don't get into conspiracy theories. There's some weird stuff out there. I just go, 'You know what? Let's just stay on course.' Don't go over here and way over there. I'm telling you, you can find a devil behind every bush if you're looking for one. There's a lot of weird stuff out there, and I go, 'You know what? If they got people born again and they turn people to Jesus, I'm on your side.' Let's just stay on the side of the body of Christ and be all for each other and pray for each other. Now, I've got a lot of haters, but you know what? I look at them and say, 'We pray for them.' They make videos of me, and I say, 'Listen, we're going to love on them anyway.' It doesn't matter what people say about you; it's what you allow inside, how you feel about them. I'm not going to get bitter; I refuse to walk out of love. You may not agree with what I say, but the Bible says, 'Touch not mine anointed, and do my prophets no harm.' Don't touch people with your mouth, regardless of what they say about you, what they feel about you, or how they act. That's not my responsibility. My job is to automatically walk in love. They can call me a false prophet, they can call me all these things, but my foundation is to say, 'You know what, brother? We're praying for you. We pray for your marriage. We pray for your wife. We pray for healing and health on you and longevity of ministry over you.' Regardless of what you feel about me, if I'm not going to be buddies with you, that's fine, but I'm going to pray for you instead. It's important that we always, no matter how much people persecute the body of Christ—I catch it all from all kinds of people. The world hates me, and a lot of Christians do, but you know what? I choose to walk in love because love takes no account of a suffered wrong. You may say, 'Well, he doesn't hear God at all. He doesn't say all these things.' Whatever. I just keep on doing what God has called me to do. It's like I talked to somebody on the phone. They said, 'An apple doesn't have to announce that it's an apple. An orange doesn't have to announce it's an orange. They automatically display who they are, and they taste of what they're supposed to be.' So, a prophetic ministry is going to be a prophetic ministry, and you're going to taste and see it. But they don't have to go around announcing, 'Hey, I'm a prophet. I'm a prophet. I'm a prophet.' Because if they're always going around saying, 'I'm Prophet so-and-so. I'm Prophet this, Prophet that,' you don't have to announce it. Your fruit of who you are in your ministry is automatically going to display when you show up, because you carry that anointing. You're a carrier of it. Aren't you right, baby? And it's like Billy Burke doesn't have to come around and tell everybody, 'I'm a healing evangelist. Glory to God.' You see the fruit of who he is when he walks in and comes up on the platform. Bam! The atmosphere changes, and people's lives are getting changed and they're getting healed, and you can see the authenticity. The Holy Spirit leads and guides you to know and to see visibly the ones who are authentic versus those who are pretenders. I don't have to tell you I'm an orange if I'm an orange. You're going to taste and see it. And there are a lot of people who try to tell you and convince you that they're an apple when they're not an apple—they're a plum, and you're going, 'That doesn't line up right,' because signs will follow them that believe. Signs follow the anointing that's on your life. And so if you don't see the anointing of a watchman on me, then just go on. I don't care. I'm just going to keep doing it. And if the Lord corrects me and says, 'Brandon, you're off,' then I'm going to say, 'Okay, forgive me, Lord, but I want to help people.' Whatever I've got to do, let's just do it for all the glory of God. 'All that you do, in word and in deed, do all to the glory of God.' Just like Pastor Chip is always talking. Brother Hagin told him, 'Chip, if you want to stay on target...' How to sow into good ground... Brother Hagin told our pastor, Pastor Chip, when we go to our church there, he said, 'If you don't ever want to get off in life and get off on the wrong road, this is the main key that you need to focus on: in all that you do, in word and in deed, do all to the glory of God, and you'll not get off track.' Everything I do, I'm doing in word and in deed; I do all for the glory of God. So if they don't agree with what you're doing and they want to throw eggs at you, say, 'I'm just doing all to the glory of God. I'm not doing this for me, brother. I'm doing all to the glory of God.' He said, 'If I be lifted up, I'll draw all men unto me.' Because, like Billy Burke said, it's 'to attract, to deflect.' I'm not here to take the glory for myself in anything on this YouTube channel. And the more it grows, we give all the glory to God. And when you do that, you stay on track because you're giving all glory to Him. It's all glory to You, Lord. We give all the glory to Jesus and tell of His love. We give all of the glory to Jesus. That's where it's at. I feel like people need to hear this. So, when you're sowing into certain ministries and you're believing for your financial harvest, look at where you're sowing. We test ground. Diana and I, we will test it and we'll sow there. And if we don't see fruit, you don't keep sowing there; that's stupid. Don't do that; that is dumb because of the devourer. You go, 'There's something wrong with this ground.' And I'm telling you, not all ground produces, does it? It doesn't all produce. There are times we've given in the past to certain ground, and the devourer comes right knocking at your door, and you go, 'That is not God. Repent. There's something wrong.' Sow somewhere else. Poof! I mean seriously, because you go, 'There's something off with this ground,' and you know because the ground where you're sowing will produce. So we automatically test it. But we know that he that winneth souls is wise. We know that prayer warriors and end-time harvesters—nothing happens in the natural unless it happens in the supernatural first. You must birth it in the spirit in prayer, like on these channels. Prayer changes things. You must birth it in prayer so that you can have it in the natural, because the harvest of souls that we're believing for in this massive revival will be birthed out of prayer. Azusa Street was birthed out of prayer through William Seymour. They had prayer meetings all the time. With John Kilpatrick and the Pensacola Revival, they birthed it out of prayer for two years, having prayer services in there. With Lynne Hammond's church—the one up there that I'm talking about when they had their little revival up there—they birthed it out of prayer. Everything was birthed out of great intercessory prayer first. It doesn't just happen. That's what I was telling Billy Burke. I said, 'We've got to have more intercessory prayer the more this day goes on and on.' Even Kathryn Kuhlman, when she first started out, she had a sign that said, 'Prayer changes things.' And I just found that out. We didn't even know it. It was a light up, and she put it over the door. It was just a little light up sign that said, "Prayer changes things." And she would go in there with whoever the girl was that she started the ministry with, her friend, her sister or cousin or something like that, right? I think it was a family member. It might have just been a friend, but they went in there and prayed is what I read. I thought, "Wow." She even said, "Prayer changes things." And was that her foundation name or something? Yeah, her ministry. It wasn't foundation; it was just "Prayer Changes Things." But then she started something. I don't know, it was interesting. But I said, "Diana, that's what we got by the Spirit of God to call our stuff: Prayer Changes Things." Yeah, and that's why, because we believe it. The only thing that's ever going to get you from glory to glory is prayer and fellowship with the Father. And this word, this beautiful book—people go, you know, you get off into weirdo land because you're always trying to listen. You got people listening to fortune tellers on here. Is it okay if I go to these? No. Stay away from soothsayers and fortune tellers, tarot card readers. It's witchcraft. Don't do that. You go to the Word. If you want God to give you a word, read your Bible, find it, and He will speak to you. This Word will change you from the inside out. Yes, this is an old book, but it's alive. Do you understand? It's alive. The Word of God is alive, and it's sharper than any two-edged sword. It will change you from the inside out. And this Word will literally—the words will jump out of the pages if you go to it with communion, praise, and thanksgiving: "Father, I thank You that You're changing me and taking me from glory to glory. I need a fresh word from You today." And a lot of times I read Psalms or Proverbs or whatever, and you find your Proverbs of the day, your Psalms. Proverbs, you can find, you read all the chapter 31 times in the Proverbs, and 31 days out of a month, you can get something good out of those Proverbs. Find your daily Proverbs. My go-to lately is John. I start in John 14 and I go all the way to John 17, and I feel like I'm just sitting under the water faucet and I'm just drinking, drinking, drinking. Those passages are just amazing to me lately. So, I highly suggest reading those if you are led to, because it's amazing to get something fresh. Oh, the red. It's the red. Get the red. I always find the red. "Living by the Words of Jesus." I'm looking, I say, if we don't know what Jesus said, then what are we doing? Because He was the one we're following after anyway. I follow all the red. Read the red. Take a week and just read all the red, and meditate on it. Don't just read it. It's like the Lord told her, He said, "Now, you read through the Bible all through this year. Now, I want you to read, not just going through it as fast as you can to read all of it, but study it and meditate on it." Yes, so it becomes rhema. He said, "I want you to read, not just to read. Read so it penetrates your spirit and you read as you're led." So when I'm reading, I'll be reading, and all of a sudden the Holy Spirit will highlight a passage to me, and that's when I slow down and I go back over it over and over, like I'm eating, like I'm sticking my knife and fork in, and I'm just digging into that verse. It could be just one verse, but I will just dig into that one verse and I can get a whole big thing, and the Holy Spirit's just speaking to me as I'm reading that. So, it's been amazing. Yep, that's what she does. That's what we all should do. You all know that I've had problems with reading a lot because I've had learning disabilities. So, Diana reads to me. Whatever I'm telling you, I read it and I go over it, but for my comprehension, I depend on her a lot for a lot of things. So, she reads over it, and I listen to my Bible. I put it next to me and I listen to it. If I read it, I understand it and I get it, but I get a lot when I'm listening. So, I put it on there and I read it and I follow along with that Bible app, and it reads for me, and that's how I grasp the scriptures. But she's a bookworm, so she reads a lot to me, and so that's why I always pull on her. I can listen to it, but for some reason, there's something about reading it, and when I read it and I'm actually looking at it, for me, it's just completely different. Everybody's different as learners, in the way they see things and hear things. Whenever I get my eyes on the red, I just let it transform me. Yep. And she gets those highlighters. She is over there highlighting and coloring. She uses her Bible, I told her, as a coloring book. Somebody sent her some more highlighters the other day; that was such a blessing. And she said, "Hey, I've already—" She has highlighters and she gets these highlighters... "I wear them down and I wear them out so fast. I go through them really, really quick." You use your coloring book, but it's a blessing. I just try to tell you all things that help us, that may help you, you know. Not that we have it all together, but I'm going to tell you this: we try. We're trying. And as we go from glory to glory and what the Lord's shown us, He comes and visits us. And it's like Diana said—we talked about it the other day—she goes, "We can't afford in the natural just to listen to Sundays and Wednesdays. We are people that have to be in the Word." There's no more sitting on the couch and veging out and watching our sitcoms or whatever we used to in the past, eating popcorn and watching things, sitting there and going, "This is our time to veg." We can't afford to do that anymore. No, it's like we're drug addicts; we go from one fix to the next. We're like, "That didn't get that scratch." And so we'll go to the next thing and we'll keep listening and listening to sermons until we find the right one. And we're like, "Oh, that was it! That was it! That was it!" And we'll pick it like a chicken on a bone. We'll go and pick through that thing. I told her, I said, "We're scavengers. We don't depend on our pastors to feed us spiritually. You don't need to depend on your pastors to feed you spiritually." How desperate are you for your life to change? If you're waiting on your pastor to preach a sermon behind the pulpit to change your life, you're missing it. You go there to just get a refilling. You just get a little bit extra, a nugget. You glean from it, and you listen and you know that you're staying on track. You have some spiritual somebody there to help you out, and you get refreshed just being in that atmosphere. Yeah, being together—they're family. But I don't go to our church to get spiritually fed. No. If I'm waiting for them to give me something spiritually on just Sundays or Wednesdays, I'm going to starve. By that time, you're going to be anemic. Because I go, "Man, and that's not fair to them." "Well, you know what? That meal wasn't very good, Pastor. I'm in trouble until next week." We eat twice a week? No, no way. There's so many people that are doing that. And that's why I think if it was—in fact, when I go there, I'm already full. Yeah, I'm already full. I'm going, Diana needs to pat me on my back because spiritually I'm perfect because I've already eaten all day long. And I'm like, "Where are we going tonight?" Oh, yeah. Because we're worshiping the Lord. We're giving Him some time. We're honoring Him. But I'm not going for Pastor Chip and Candace to make sure they feed us or whatever, because we're dumping out on this channel all the time. And we go, "What do we do?" But what I'm trying to tell you all is that we find it for ourselves. We're scavengers. And it may just look like, you know, like they make that old gumbo and it looks like something that came out of a ditch, but I'm going to take it, and it's going to be good by the time we're done. You know what I'm saying? We're going to find it. Wherever it is, we're going to find it. We're going to pick the bones of this thing. And we pray. I pray every time I go on and I'm trying to search for a sermon—a good sermon, a Kenneth Hagin sermon, or whatever it is. I pray before and I say, "Holy Spirit, lead me. Lead me to the right one." I don't want to just listen to like five sermons that were just mediocre and finally on the sixth one, it's the one I want. Because there are some of them out there, even with them. Time is of the essence, and we don't always have a lot of time during our day. So, we have to make it count. She has found some honey. I mean, I'm telling you, I told her, "You've found some good ones today, babe." We sat in the car; I didn't want to get out of the car. I told her, "I started weeping. Hold on a minute. This one right here—can we rewind that?" And she's like, "Fast forward, fast forward." And we listened to it again. And I said, "Oh, take notes on it." She got this little thing—it's a little recorder. She pushes the button when the Lord's giving me a word now. She has to have her phone; she goes "bing" and she gets those words recorded because there are nuggets that will change your life if you grab onto them. When you value the Word, when the Word becomes everything you have, "I need this. I'm desperate. I have to have a touch from the Father today." And that's why she said we can't do those binge-watching of certain things that made us laugh in the past. We have to find the living Word because of where we are in these end times. This is how serious it is. This is wartime. The more you give into that tugging, that Holy Ghost tugging when He tugs you throughout the day, and whenever you listen to it and yield to that tugging, saying, "Okay, it's time to listen. It's time to put some Word on. Time to read the Bible," the more you do it, it changes you. And I don't want to be the same person next year that I am right now. I want to constantly be changing. We're never going to say we've achieved it all, that we are where we're supposed to be spiritually, or that we're more spiritual than anybody else. We're never going to be that person, but we're always going to strive. I press on in this race of the high calling to finish my course, like that scripture says: "I have fought the good fight, I have finished my course, I have kept the faith." I refuse to get up there and... What were you going to say? I was going to say, and sometimes I feel like maybe I need a little bit more than most people. Maybe I'm a little more clingy. Maybe I'm like, "Lord, I need You so much right now." One little thing in my day and I'm like, "Daddy, I need help today. This has been rough." And I just cling. So, that's just how I get by. It's on Him. Being filled with and controlled by the Holy Ghost, being desperate for that feeling. I need You right now, and I can't do this life without You. I don't know how anybody can do it. The darker it gets, how are they able to do life without Jesus? That's why maybe they are talking to ChatGPT and trying to find stuff and trying to find that inward peace, because they don't have anything else. But it's like I heard Billy Burke say in one of his sermons, he was talking about how whenever he's walking around... He said that's why people go to Disney World and that's why people go to concerts: there's a presence and they're trying to fill that void. They go there because that's what they're trying to serve. They don't even ride the rides. He said, "I live by there and we go there and just walk around and we watch people, and ninety percent of them don't ever even get on a ride. They just want to feel the atmosphere of Disney World." And then they go to a football game because they're trying to submerge themselves in that atmosphere, but it only lasts for an hour or two, and then it's over. And then what are you left with? So, it's just people throughout the world constantly trying to get into a presence that fills something in them. That's why we wanted to make a place here for people to come and drink, for people to come and get refreshed on a day-to-day basis, because we know what it's like to be a scavenger and go. Maybe this could be your oasis. I told her, "Maybe we could be the oasis for them, that we bring an atmosphere, a place for them to get refreshed, that they come and they hear there's hope." Yeah, there's judgment, these things the Lord's showing me that are not too fun sometimes, but it's still telling you that Jesus Christ is coming back soon, and He has a plan for your life to bless you, to heal you, to prosper you, to give you an expected future and hope, and that we can pray together and change things through prayer. I just believe that when I'm telling you about these grounds, people don't know this stuff. We've done this through trial and error. I'm forty-five, and it doesn't matter how old she is. She's young. I'm teasing with you. It's nobody's business, right? But I'm just saying, that's what Kathryn Kuhlman says: we don't tell women's age. But I'm saying there's a lot of people that are desperate and they're wanting just a touch from the Lord. They come to that place of dryness and they say, "God, where are you in this dryness?" And that's why I've tried to teach you my nuggets of where we've learned to sew, how we've learned to do things, and it works for us, and we see fruit. But a lot of people keep doing the same thing over and over again, and they're still broke. They're not going anywhere spiritually, and they're expecting different results.
